# @ledewire/node

> LedeWire Node.js SDK — full API surface for merchant stores, seller content, and buyer flows.
> npm install @ledewire/node
> Source: https://github.com/ledewire/ledewire-js-sdk
> API docs: https://ledewire.github.io/ledewire-js-sdk/api/

## Install

```bash
npm install @ledewire/node
```

## Quick start

```ts
import { createClient } from '@ledewire/node'

// API key + secret — write access for seller/merchant operations
const client = createClient({
  apiKey: process.env.LEDEWIRE_API_KEY,
  apiSecret: process.env.LEDEWIRE_API_SECRET,
})

// Merchant JWT auth (no API key needed)
const client = createClient()
const { stores } = await client.merchant.auth.loginWithEmailAndListStores({
  email: 'owner@example.com',
  password: process.env.MERCHANT_PASSWORD,
})
const storeId = stores[0].id  // MerchantLoginStore — use .id (not .store_id)
```

## createClient() configuration

```ts
import { createClient } from '@ledewire/node'
import type { NodeClientConfig } from '@ledewire/node'

const client = createClient({
  // Optional — omit for merchant JWT-only flows
  apiKey: string,
  apiSecret: string,          // omit for read-only seller access

  baseUrl: string,            // defaults to 'https://api.ledewire.com'

  // Token persistence (required for serverless/edge — in-memory by default)
  storage: {
    getTokens: () => Promise<StoredTokens | null>,
    setTokens: (t: StoredTokens) => Promise<void>,  // fires on login AND refresh
    clearTokens: () => Promise<void>,
  },

  // Optional — fires ONLY on background token refresh (not on login).
  // Use for side-effects only (audit logging, cache invalidation).
  // Do NOT use for persistence — storage.setTokens already handles that.
  // Providing both storage and onTokenRefreshed will double-write on refresh.
  onTokenRefreshed: (tokens: StoredTokens) => Promise<void> | void,
  onAuthExpired: () => void,  // called when refresh fails — re-authenticate
})
```

Token refresh is automatic — never call a refresh method manually.

> **Serverless / edge:** MemoryTokenStorage (default) resets on every cold start.
> Always provide a custom `storage` adapter (Redis, database, encrypted cookie).

## Field naming convention

All API response types use `snake_case` field names (`access_token`, `price_cents`,
`store_key`), matching the wire format exactly — what you see in a network inspector
matches your TypeScript type.

SDK-owned types that do not map 1:1 to a raw API response use `camelCase`:
- `StoredTokens` — `accessToken`, `refreshToken`, `expiresAt: number` (already
  converted from ISO string; not the same thing as `expires_at: string`)
- `MerchantLoginResult` — `tokens: StoredTokens`, `stores: MerchantLoginStore[]`
- `PaginationParams` — `page`, `perPage`

Do not attempt to remap snake_case response fields to camelCase — the SDK
does not offer a `transformKeys` option and is not designed for one.

## Complete method reference

### client.config

```ts
client.config.getPublic() → Promise<PublicConfigResponse>
// { google_client_id: string, ... }
// Safe to call before authentication — no credentials required.
```

### client.auth (buyer auth)

```ts
client.auth.signup(body)                                             → Promise<AuthenticationResponse>
client.auth.loginWithEmail({ email, password })                      → Promise<AuthenticationResponse>
client.auth.loginWithGoogle({ id_token })                            → Promise<AuthenticationResponse>
client.auth.loginWithBuyerApiKey({ key, secret })                    → Promise<AuthenticationResponse>
client.auth.loginWithApiKey({ key, secret? })                        → Promise<AuthenticationResponse>
client.auth.logout()                                                 → Promise<void>
client.auth.refreshToken()                                           → Promise<AuthenticationResponse>
client.auth.requestPasswordReset({ email })                          → Promise<void>
client.auth.resetPassword({ email, reset_code, password })           → Promise<void>
```

`loginWithBuyerApiKey` is intended for agents and buyer-facing dashboards that
authenticate headlessly using a named API key. The token is stored automatically.
See `client.user.apiKeys.create()` to generate a key.

### client.merchant.auth

```ts
client.merchant.auth.loginWithEmail({ email, password })     → Promise<MerchantAuthenticationResponse>
client.merchant.auth.loginWithGoogle({ id_token })           → Promise<MerchantAuthenticationResponse>
client.merchant.auth.listStores()                            → Promise<ManageableStore[]>
client.merchant.auth.loginWithEmailAndListStores({ email, password }) → Promise<MerchantLoginResult>
client.merchant.auth.loginWithGoogleAndListStores({ id_token })       → Promise<MerchantLoginResult>
client.merchant.auth.requestPasswordReset({ email })         → Promise<MerchantPasswordResetResponse>
client.merchant.auth.resetPassword({ email, reset_code, password }) → Promise<MerchantPasswordResetResponse>
```

`MerchantLoginResult = { tokens: StoredTokens, stores: MerchantLoginStore[] }`
// StoredTokens = { accessToken: string, refreshToken: string, expiresAt: number (Unix ms) }
// tokens are already normalized — no remapping or parseExpiresAt() call needed

**`MerchantLoginStore` vs `ManageableStore`:**

Both types share the same `id`/`name` field names for the store identifier and display name.
`ManageableStore` (from `listStores()`) additionally carries `.store_key`, `.is_author`, and `.logo`.

| Type | Source | Fields |
|---|---|---|
| `MerchantLoginStore` | Embedded in login response (combo helpers) | `.id`, `.name`, `.role` |
| `ManageableStore` | Returned by `listStores()` (second HTTP call) | `.id`, `.name`, `.store_key`, `.role`, `.is_author`, `.logo` |

```ts
// Combo helper (recommended — single HTTP call)
const { stores } = await client.merchant.auth.loginWithEmailAndListStores(...)
stores[0].id        // ✅ MerchantLoginStore.id

// Separate listStores call (use only when you need store_key / logo)
await client.merchant.auth.loginWithEmail(...)
const stores = await client.merchant.auth.listStores()
stores[0].id        // ✅ ManageableStore.id — same field name
```

**Error handling for merchant login:**

```ts
import { ForbiddenError, AuthError } from '@ledewire/node'

try {
  await client.merchant.auth.loginWithGoogle({ id_token })
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof ForbiddenError) {
    // 403 — credentials are VALID but the account has no merchant store access.
    // This is the expected error when a personal Google account previously
    // registered as a buyer is used on the merchant login endpoint.
    // err.message → "This account does not have merchant access. Use a merchant or owner account."
    // Fix: use a dedicated merchant/owner Google account, or ask a store owner to add your account.
  } else if (err instanceof AuthError) {
    // 401 — bad credentials or expired token.
  }
}
```

The same `ForbiddenError` (403) vs `AuthError` (401) distinction applies to all four
merchant auth methods: `loginWithEmail`, `loginWithGoogle`, `loginWithEmailAndListStores`,
`loginWithGoogleAndListStores`.

### client.merchant.users

```ts
client.merchant.users.list(storeId, params?)   → Promise<PaginatedUsersList>
client.merchant.users.invite(storeId, body)    → Promise<MerchantUser>
client.merchant.users.remove(storeId, userId)  → Promise<void>
client.merchant.users.update(storeId, userId, body: MerchantUserUpdateRequest) → Promise<MerchantUser>
// MerchantUserUpdateRequest: { author_fee_bps?: number | null }
// author_fee_bps in basis points (1800 = 18%). null reverts to store default.
```

### client.merchant.sales

```ts
client.merchant.sales.summary(storeId)              → Promise<SalesSummaryResponse>
client.merchant.sales.list(storeId, params?)         → Promise<PaginatedSalesList>
client.merchant.sales.get(storeId, saleId)           → Promise<MerchantSaleResponse>
client.merchant.sales.statistics(storeId, params?)   → Promise<{ data: SalesStatisticsItem[], pagination: PaginationMeta }>
```

### client.merchant.buyers

```ts
client.merchant.buyers.list(storeId, params?)  → Promise<PaginatedBuyersList>
```

### client.merchant.config

```ts
client.merchant.config.get(storeId)            → Promise<StoreConfig>
client.merchant.config.update(storeId, body)   → Promise<StoreConfig>
```

### client.merchant.pricingRules

Manage x402 URL-based pricing rules. The domain in `url_pattern` must be verified
via `client.merchant.domains` before a rule can be created.

```ts
client.merchant.pricingRules.list(storeId)              → Promise<MerchantPricingRule[]>
client.merchant.pricingRules.create(storeId, body)      → Promise<MerchantPricingRule>
client.merchant.pricingRules.deactivate(storeId, ruleId) → Promise<MerchantPricingRule>
```

### client.merchant.domains

Verify domain ownership required for x402 pricing rules. Add a domain to receive
DNS TXT record instructions; the platform verifies asynchronously.

```ts
client.merchant.domains.list(storeId)               → Promise<MerchantDomainVerification[]>
client.merchant.domains.add(storeId, body)          → Promise<MerchantDomainVerification>
client.merchant.domains.remove(storeId, domainId)   → Promise<void>
```

### client.merchant.content

```ts
client.merchant.content.list(storeId, params?)         → Promise<PaginatedContentList>
client.merchant.content.get(storeId, contentId)        → Promise<ContentResponse>
client.merchant.content.create(storeId, body)          → Promise<ContentResponse>
client.merchant.content.update(storeId, contentId, body) → Promise<ContentResponse>
client.merchant.content.delete(storeId, contentId)     → Promise<void>
client.merchant.content.search(storeId, query, params?) → Promise<PaginatedContentList>
```

### client.seller.content

```ts
client.seller.content.list(storeId, params?)            → Promise<PaginatedContentList>
client.seller.content.get(storeId, contentId)           → Promise<ContentResponse>
client.seller.content.create(storeId, body)             → Promise<ContentResponse>
client.seller.content.update(storeId, contentId, body)  → Promise<ContentResponse>
client.seller.content.delete(storeId, contentId)        → Promise<void>
client.seller.content.search(storeId, query, params?)   → Promise<PaginatedContentList>
```

### client.wallet

```ts
client.wallet.balance()                            → Promise<WalletBalanceResponse>
client.wallet.transactions(params?)                → Promise<{ data: WalletTransactionItem[], pagination: PaginationMeta }>
client.wallet.createPaymentSession(body)           → Promise<WalletPaymentSessionResponse>
client.wallet.getPaymentStatus(sessionId)          → Promise<WalletPaymentStatusResponse>
```

### client.purchases

```ts
client.purchases.create({ content_id: string })   → Promise<PurchaseResponse>
client.purchases.list(params?)                    → Promise<{ data: PurchaseResponse[], pagination: PaginationMeta }>
client.purchases.get(purchaseId)                  → Promise<PurchaseResponse>
```

### client.content

```ts
client.content.getWithAccess(contentId, userId?) → Promise<ContentWithAccessResponse>
// userId: optional buyer ID for merchant server-side proxy lookups
```

### client.checkout

```ts
client.checkout.state(contentId)                  → Promise<CheckoutStateResponse>
```

### client.user

Manage buyer API keys. Keys are used with `auth.loginWithBuyerApiKey()` for
agent authentication. The secret is shown **once** at creation — store it
immediately in a secrets manager.

```ts
client.user.apiKeys.list()                         → Promise<UserApiKey[]>
client.user.apiKeys.create(body)                   → Promise<UserApiKeyCreateResponse>
client.user.apiKeys.revoke(keyId)                  → Promise<void>
```

```ts
// body for create:
interface UserApiKeyCreateRequest {
  name: string                     // human-readable label e.g. 'my-rag-agent'
  spending_limit_cents?: number    // optional per-period cap in cents
}

// list response item (no secret):
interface UserApiKey {
  id: string
  name: string
  key: string                      // e.g. 'bktst_abc123' — share with agent
  last_used_at: string | null
  spending_limit_cents: number | null
  created_at: string
}

// create response (secret visible once only):
interface UserApiKeyCreateResponse extends UserApiKey {
  secret: string  // 64-char hex — STORE IMMEDIATELY, never shown again
}
```

## createAgentClient()

A convenience factory for autonomous (headless) agents. Wraps `createClient()`
and exposes only the buyer-relevant namespaces: `auth`, `wallet`, `purchases`,
`content`, `checkout`, `user`.

```ts
import { createAgentClient } from '@ledewire/node'
import type { AgentClientConfig, AgentClient } from '@ledewire/node'

const agent = createAgentClient({
  key: process.env.LEDEWIRE_BUYER_KEY,    // 'bktst_...' from user.apiKeys.create()
  secret: process.env.LEDEWIRE_BUYER_SECRET,  // 64-char hex, shown once
  // storage: myRedisAdapter,  // required for serverless — default is MemoryTokenStorage
})

// Step 1: authenticate
await agent.auth.loginWithBuyerApiKey({
  key: process.env.LEDEWIRE_BUYER_KEY,
  secret: process.env.LEDEWIRE_BUYER_SECRET,
})

// Step 2: use wallet, purchases, content, checkout normally
const balance = await agent.wallet.balance()
const purchase = await agent.purchases.create({ content_id: 'abc123' })
const article  = await agent.content.getWithAccess(purchase.content_id)
```

`AgentClient` is typed as `Pick<NodeClient, 'auth' | 'wallet' | 'purchases' | 'content' | 'checkout' | 'user'>`.
The `merchant`, `seller`, and `config` namespaces are intentionally absent.

**Serverless warning:** `MemoryTokenStorage` (default) resets on every cold start.
In serverless or edge environments always provide a persistent `storage` adapter
so the agent doesn't re-authenticate on every invocation.

## Paginated responses

All list methods return `{ data, pagination }` — never plain arrays:

```ts
import type { PaginationParams, PaginationMeta } from '@ledewire/node'

const { data, pagination } = await client.seller.content.list(storeId, {
  page: 2,
  per_page: 20,
})
// pagination: { total, per_page, current_page, total_pages, next_page, prev_page }

// Affected methods: merchant.users.list, merchant.sales.list, merchant.buyers.list,
// seller.content.list, seller.content.search, merchant.content.list,
// merchant.content.search, wallet.transactions, purchases.list
```

## Key types

```ts
interface ContentListItem {
  id: string
  content_type: 'markdown' | 'external_ref'
  title: string
  price_cents: number
  teaser: string | null       // plain text teaser (SDK decodes base64 automatically); null when not set
  visibility: 'public' | 'unlisted' | 'private'
  created_at: string          // ISO 8601
  content_uri: string | null  // external URL for external_ref items; null for markdown
  external_identifier?: string | null  // e.g. 'vimeo:987654321' — NOT a URL
}
// content_uri is safe to use as <a href> directly — no conversion or extra fetch needed
```

```ts
interface CheckoutStateResponse {
  content_id: string
  content_title: string
  price_cents: number
  checkout_state: {
    is_authenticated: boolean
    has_sufficient_funds: boolean | null
    has_purchased: boolean
    next_required_action: 'authenticate' | 'fund_wallet' | 'purchase' | 'view_content'
  }
}

interface StoredTokens {
  accessToken: string
  refreshToken: string
  expiresAt: number  // Unix timestamp (ms)
}

interface TokenStorage {
  getTokens(): Promise<StoredTokens | null>
  setTokens(tokens: StoredTokens): Promise<void>
  clearTokens(): Promise<void>
}
```

## Error classes

All errors extend `LedewireError`. Always catch by subclass for actionable handling:

```ts
import {
  LedewireError,
  AuthError,
  ForbiddenError,
  NotFoundError,
  PurchaseError,
} from '@ledewire/node'

class LedewireError extends Error {
  statusCode: number        // HTTP status code
  code: number | undefined  // machine-readable code from API body
  message: string           // human-readable description from API body
}

class AuthError extends LedewireError {}       // 401 — bad credentials / expired token
class ForbiddenError extends LedewireError {}  // 403 — authenticated but insufficient access
class NotFoundError extends LedewireError {}   // 404 — resource not found
class PurchaseError extends LedewireError {}   // 409/422 — purchase validation failure
```

## Testing with mock client

```ts
// ONLY import from the /testing subpath — never from the main entry point.
// This subpath is excluded from production bundles.
import { createMockClient } from '@ledewire/node/testing'
```

**Per-method mock control** (recommended — works with Vitest and Jest):

```ts
const client = createMockClient(vi.fn)

// Wrap individual methods with vi.mocked() to access MockInstance types:
vi.mocked(client.merchant.auth.loginWithEmail).mockResolvedValue(tokenFixture)
vi.mocked(client.seller.content.list).mockResolvedValue({ data: [], pagination: { ... } })
expect(vi.mocked(client.merchant.sales.list).mock.calls).toHaveLength(1)
```

**Whole-client deep mock** (Vitest only — surfaces MockInstance on every method without
per-call `vi.mocked()` wrappers):

```ts
// vi.mocked(..., true) casts all nested functions to Mock<...> at once.
// Without the `true` flag (or without vi.mocked at all), TypeScript reports:
//   Property 'mockResolvedValue' does not exist on type '(...) => Promise<...>'
const client = vi.mocked(createMockClient(vi.fn), true)

client.merchant.auth.loginWithEmail.mockResolvedValue(tokenFixture)
client.seller.content.list.mockResolvedValue({ data: [], pagination: { ... } })
```
